input.prompt:
I need to find a specific fact from the MTEB (Massive Text Embedding Benchmark) Scandinavian leaderboard.

Question: According to the Scandinavian MTEB leaderboard, as of August 2025, which embedding model has the highest "Mean (Task)" score, considering ONLY models that have results for ALL tasks in the benchmark (no missing task results)?

The MTEB leaderboard is hosted on HuggingFace Spaces at https://huggingface.co/spaces/mteb/leaderboard . There is a "Scandinavian" benchmark/tab. Note that the leaderboard is an interactive app; you may need to look at the underlying data. The MTEB results are stored in the GitHub repo https://github.com/embeddings-benchmark/results and the benchmark definitions in https://github.com/embeddings-benchmark/mteb . 

Please:
1. Determine the exact list of tasks in the "MTEB(Scandinavian)" or Scandinavian benchmark as of ~August 2025.
2. Find which models have results for ALL those tasks.
3. Among those, identify the one with the highest Mean (Task) score.

Report the answer as the model name in "organization/model_name" format (e.g., "intfloat/multilingual-e5-large"). Provide your confidence and the sources you used. Also list the top 3-5 candidate models with their Mean (Task) scores if possible.

import mteb
b = mteb.get_benchmark('MTEB(Scandinavian, v1)')
tasks = list(b.tasks)
task_names = set(t.metadata.name for t in tasks)
n_expected = len(task_names)

res = mteb.load_results(tasks=tasks, download_latest=False, require_model_meta=False, validate_and_filter=True)
print('type', type(res))
# res is BenchmarkResults; iterate model results
rows=[]
for mr in res.model_results:
    scored = {}
    for tr in mr.task_results:
        if tr.task_name in task_names:
            try:
                s = tr.get_score()
            except Exception as e:
                continue
            scored[tr.task_name]=s
    rows.append((mr.model_name, len(scored), scored))

print('total models', len(rows))
complete=[r for r in rows if r[1]==n_expected]
print('complete models', len(complete))
import statistics
res2=[]
for name,cnt,scored in complete:
    mean=sum(scored.values())/len(scored)
    res2.append((mean,name))
res2.sort(reverse=True)
for mean,name in res2[:10]:
    print(f'{mean*100:.2f}  {name}')
